item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not very original, and just kinda being complex for the sake of complexity. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

I think the -A is the media outlet, not the image itself.
I think that its a thing that makes the owner successful if the -A is successful
I think the monkey's paw is that it also makes everyone who knows about the -A know about the owner.
Obv this is a problem for the foundation should it breach. Brilliant.
-1 cause this was really hard to read.
+1 cause I think you deliberately wrote it that way.
+1 cause its a thing that does a thing (I like things that do things)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It just feels like generic Dirtbag Left memery, just anomalous and with an interesting mechanism that's still underused. I dunno, maybe I've been around Chapo and Cumtown too much, but it doesn't evoke anything other than 'well at least someone got off Twitter to really fight The Man".

item.title: Meh
item.content:encoded: Maybe it's just me, but I felt like this one wasn't written out very well. I mean, it was kind of interesting, but also fairly poorly explained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i will never understand why The Volgun decided to read this. it's not like i'm mad, just confused.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do just wanna say, real quick, that I'm sorry. Sorry that I can't do anything except mess with the media while the planet's burning, obviously, but also I'm sorry for forcing the issue.

You know, that just seems like terrible planning. If success and possession are correlated, then by getting on television, his success is pre-ordained, and then people will listen to his manifesto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is one jaundice-ass eye. I'd see that eye and be like, "let's find you a liver today."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know I had to do it to them

Fantastic. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't worry about it. It's more to just demonstrate that things get really mixed up.

The functional purpose of this endeavor is to get pictures of SCP-???? on as many media outlets as possible; PoI-0 originally thought to take the time to give a "manifesto" of sorts, but they decided people wouldn't care about that, so they settle for apologizing for what they're about to do and how it will force people to deal with uncomfortable truths. The video log shows Poi-0 burning down an army recruitment center, saying some provocative things, and then putting their face (which contains SCP-????) on camera while daring people to upload the video to their websites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't figure out the last half of this article. I got the description, but the part of the Recovery section that provides all the context is (deliberately) word salad and I'm struggling to pick it apart. :

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a strict internal logic that's only slightly out of the reader's grasp

wow well said

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i really liked what i understood from this. 1+ i might draw it some time if i remember too.

edit: i gone and dunnit, love that log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really liked this one. The feeling of a strict internal logic that's only slightly out of the reader's grasp is something that comes across (for me at least) a lot in your work, and this is a perfect example of that. The puzzle pieces are there, and although I can't put all of them together I can see enough to tell it makes a very pretty picture, and that's more than enough to seal an upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I read this, I could tell the author had an advanced sense of literary composition and abstract thought, but I was pulled out of my immersion due to several uncharacteristic terminological redundancies.

+ show block
– hide block

“Containment operations are considered finished once SCP-4018-A is no longer operational”
“Its construction is consistent with similar models constructed in Europe in the 17th century.”
“This effect makes it difficult to ascertain the exact nature of the correlation between control SCP-4018-A's control over SCP-4018 and its success,”

But these are easy fixes and the article is enjoyably puzzling, really worth a mull or two. I love the idea of anomalous ontological equivocation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this one feels a bit melodramatic, that's because it was frustrating to write. Anyways, enjoy.

EDIT: Here's the image source https://wellcomeimages.org/indexplus/image/L0058730.html That site is down right now so I can't get more specific, but that's the right source.
